macro_segments.title: Ribbit Through a Non-Jew: Shifting Loan Obligations Between Jews and Non-Jews

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Lending Money Between Jews and Non-Jews with Interest
Case 1 - Jew Borrows from Non-Jew with Interest, Third Party Offers to Pay
Two Scenarios: Paying Through Intermediary vs. In the Non-Jew's Presence
Permitted Method: Non-Jew Receives Payment, Then Lends to Third Party
Case 2 - Non-Jew Borrows from Jew, Parallel Analysis
Impermissible Method: Direct Transfer in Presence of Original Creditor
Conceptual Challenge: Who Really Owns the Money When It Changes Hands?
Tosafot's Question: Does Who Pays Interest Determine Loan Ownership?

macro_segments.title: The Role of Presence: Does Proximity to the Original Lender Create a New Loan?

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Question: Why Should Presence Matter If Money Goes Directly to Third Party?
No Shlichus (Agency) for Non-Jews: Rebuttal to Presence-Based Solution
Rav Huna's Answer: Money Placed on Table, Then Released to Third Party
Why Presence Alone Doesn't Work: The Requirement of Full Payment First
Rav Ashi's Creative Solution: A Jew CAN Be an Agent for a Non-Jew
Rejection of Rav Ashi: Shlichus Rules Based on Terumah Precedent
Ravina's Alternative: Zechiyah (Acquisition) Instead of Shlichus
Rejection of Zechiyah: Limited to Those Who Could Eventually Make Shlichus

macro_segments.title: Conversion and Ribbit: When a Non-Jewish Debtor Converts to Judaism

micro_segments.title: Scenario: Non-Jew Borrows with Interest, Becomes Jewish, Debt Reconsolidated
First Case: Loan Consolidated Before Conversion – Full Amount Payable
Second Case: Loan Consolidated After Conversion – Principal Only Payable
Analysis: Distinguishing Interest Accrued vs. Interest Owed
Parallel Case: Jew Lends to Non-Jew, Non-Jew Converts
Rabbi Yossi's Position: Non-Jewish Borrower Always Pays Full Amount
Rava's Rationale: Preventing Conversion as Tax Avoidance

macro_segments.title: Written Ribbit Contracts: Validity and Enforcement in Court

micro_segments.title: Document with Interest Written: Complete Nullification vs. Partial Enforcement
Rabbi Meir: Entire Document Voided Due to Embedded Aveirah
Chachamim: Principal Collectible, Interest Not Collectible
Underlying Debate: Can We Fine the Permitted for the Forbidden?
Comparison to Predated Documents: Why Predated Shtar Entirely Voided
Distinction: Predated Shtar vs. Interest-Laden Shtar – Severability

macro_segments.title: The Ruse and Property Transfer: Collateral, False Sale, and Document Validity

micro_segments.title: Story Setup: Lender Takes Vineyard as Collateral, Threatens Fraudulent Purchase Claim
Debtor's Counteraction: Transfer to Minor Son to Undermine Lender's Leverage
Discovery: False Sale Document Now Reveals the Ruse
Legal Question: Does False Sale Document Create Liens for Restitution?
Abaye's Comparison: Unvalidated Shtar Can Create Liens
Rava's Objection: Shtar of Sale Must Be Valid When Written
Principle of Shevach Karka'ot: Improvements vs. Principal in Disputed Sales
Final Distinction: Legitimate Sale vs. Ruse – Intent Determines Document Validity

topical_tags.term: Terumah (priestly portion - precedent for shlichus rules)
